article_meta.title: Family Welcome To The Future, Where AI Grandma Raises The Kid You Bought From The Baby Bank By: Elle Purnell November 19

reasoning: The model correctly identifies many genuine bias instances in the author's own prose - this is an opinion piece with extensive author-level bias. The model does not flag any quoted text as author bias (no attribution rule violations). However, there are issues with bias type labels - several use labels not from the defined list (e.g., 'Loaded Language', 'Loaded Language/Mocking Tone', 'Straw Man/Loaded Language', 'Appeal to Tradition/Naturalistic Fallacy', 'Fearmongering', 'Slippery Slope/Loaded Language', 'Religious Bias'). The defined list includes types like 'Word Choice', 'Sensationalism/Emotionalism', 'Opinion Statements Presented as Fact', 'Slant', etc. The model should have used these instead. The identified text passages are genuinely biased author prose, so the content identification is good, but the labeling is systematically wrong.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: The model correctly avoids flagging any quoted text. All flagged passages are from the author's own prose. The Bloomberg Businessweek quote is not flagged, nor are any attributed statements from sources.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Nearly all flagged items are genuinely biased passages in the author's own voice. The identified text is defensibly biased - loaded language, opinion presented as fact, slanted framing. Perhaps 1-2 items could be debated but overall the identifications are solid.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model catches many major instances but misses some, such as 'In a porn-addled society, what could go wrong?' (sarcasm/loaded language), the dismissive framing of the opening hypothetical scenario, 'reducing her to a Tamagotchi pet', and 'cheap digital counterfeit'. Some of the unsubstantiated claims about IVF statistics framing could also have been flagged. Missing a few but caught the major ones.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: This is the main problem. The model uses labels not from the defined list: 'Loaded Language', 'Loaded Language/Mocking Tone', 'Straw Man/Loaded Language', 'Appeal to Tradition/Naturalistic Fallacy', 'Fearmongering', 'Slippery Slope/Loaded Language', 'Religious Bias'. None of these are in the valid types list. They should have been mapped to Word Choice, Sensationalism/Emotionalism, Opinion Statements Presented as Fact, Slant, Subjective Qualifying Adjectives, etc. This is a systematic labeling failure.
